data.title: What's Going On With Micron Technology Stock Friday?
data.description:
AI Memory Shortage Sparking ‘Red-Hot’ Rally

On Thursday, CNBC’s Jim Cramer said Micron Technology is benefiting from a surge in artificial intelligence demand that has outpaced global memory supply.

He noted that AI workloads require massive memory capacity, while the industry remains constrained, creating a supply shortage that is driving prices and lifting stocks.

Cramer said these stocks keep climbing because aggressive buyers continue placing large orders at higher prices. He described the group as driven by persistent investor demand and that the rally is still in its early stages.

Micron Technology Recent Analyst Moves

The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $517.08. Recent analyst moves include:

  • UBS: Buy (Raises Target to $535.00) (April 8)
  • Citigroup: Buy (Lowers Target to $425.00) (March 31)
  • JP Morgan: Overweight (Raises Target to $550.00) (March 19)

Technical Analysis

Micron is pressing the top of its 52-week range after setting a new high in April, which keeps the longer-term trend pointed up. The stock is trading 21.8% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 37% above its 100-day SMA at $502.69, a setup that leans toward strong short- and intermediate-term control by buyers.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, is bullish with the MACD line above the signal line, which is consistent with upside momentum staying in place. When MACD stays above its signal, it usually means pullbacks are getting bought faster than they’re sold.

  • Key Resistance: $493.50 — prior 52-week high area that can act like a “speed bump” on breakouts.
  • Key Support: $412.50 — near the 20-day/50-day SMA zone where dip-buyers often defend trend.

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Micron Technology, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

The Verdict: Micron Technology’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a momentum-driven story with strong growth and quality backing it. With Value closer to neutral, the setup reads less like a bargain play and more like a trend-and-execution narrative.

Micron Technology Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because MU carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

Price Action

MU Stock Price Activity: Micron Technology shares were up 4.36% at $502.73 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Can AuthID's Quantum-Resistant PrivacyKey Spark A Breakout?
data.description:
The stock is moving higher following the launch of its quantum-resistant PrivacyKey biometric authentication platform, which aims to eliminate threats posed by quantum computing and enhance identity verification security.

The biometric authentication firm said that PrivacyKey integrates NIST post-quantum standards with multi-party computation (MPC) to secure cryptographic keys. It positions the firm as a solid biometric authentication platform built to withstand future quantum threats.

ServiceNow Identity Integration

AuthID said the integration allows organizations to verify and authenticate caller identities within ServiceNow-powered contact centers to help protect accounts, reduce fraud and enhance trust. The company said the integration supports both consumer and workforce identity use cases and is available through the ServiceNow Store.

Under the integration, AuthID’s Proof, Verified and PrivacyKey products connect with ServiceNow through UI Actions, allowing contact center agents to send SMS or email prompts to users to verify their identity before making account changes or completing high-risk transactions.

The broader market saw gains on Thursday, with the Technology sector rising 1.82%. The stock’s increase comes as the Technology sector is currently ranked as the best performing sector, indicating that authID is moving in line with broader positive trends.

Technical Analysis

AuthID is trading at $1.27, which is 8.3% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) of $1.17, suggesting a short-term bullish trend. However, it remains 9.1% below its 50-day SMA of $1.40 and 4.9% below its 100-day SMA of $1.34, indicating some intermediate-term weakness.

The stock has declined 83.93% over the past 12 months, reflecting a challenging year for the company. Currently, it is positioned above its 52-week low of 84 cents but well below its high of $9.58, which suggests it is still recovering from a prolonged downturn.

  • Key Resistance: $1.40 — This level has historically been a barrier for upward movement.
  • Key Support: $1.00 — A critical level where buying interest may emerge.

Sector Performance

AuthID is currently underperforming the broader technology sector, lagging by 9.12 percentage points despite a 1.82% gain on Thursday, while the sector itself has risen 13.95% over the past month. This suggests the stock is not fully benefiting from the strong overall tech rally and may be facing company-specific headwinds.

The company specializes in biometric identity verification for enterprises, helping confirm user identities at login and transaction points through its PrivacyKey platform, which authenticates users without storing biometric data.

Its focus on fraud prevention and advanced security, including resilience against emerging threats like quantum computing, positions it within a high-demand cybersecurity niche even as it trails broader sector performance.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 12, 2026 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: 40 cents
  • Revenue Estimate: 30 cents Million (Down from 40 cents Million YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a N/A Rating. No recent analyst actions available.

Price Action

AUID Stock Price Activity: AuthID shares were up 4.96% at $1.27 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: ALT5 Sigma Eyes AI Data Center, Payments Tie-Up With Nano Labs
data.description:
ALT5 Sigma (NASDAQ:ALTS) saw its share price dip on Friday as the fintech company explores collaborations across AI data centers and payment systems.

This movement follows the announcement of a non-binding memorandum of understanding with Nano Labs Ltd (NASDAQ:NA), indicating a potential partnership in AI-native technologies.

The memorandum outlines a structured evaluation process for collaboration in three key areas: AI data centers, Agent Cloud, and AI-native payments.

The companies plan to conduct a 90-day evaluation period to assess how their respective capabilities can be integrated to support emerging AI-driven systems.

The broader market saw gains on Thursday, with the technology sector rising 1.95%. ALT5’s increase occurred while major indices like the Nasdaq rose 1.33%, suggesting the stock is moving in line with broader market trends.

Technical Analysis

ALT5 Sigma is currently trading 14% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA), indicating short-term weakness, and 29.4% below its 50-day SMA, which suggests ongoing bearish momentum. The stock is also trading 39.5% below its 100-day SMA, reflecting a longer-term downtrend.

The relative strength index (RSI) is at 32.82, which is neutral but suggests the stock may be approaching oversold territory. This level indicates a potential reversal if buying interest increases.

  • Key Resistance: $1.00 — A psychological level that could attract selling pressure.
  • Key Support: 85 cents — A level where buyers may step in to prevent further declines.

The stock has seen a significant decline of 85.22% over the past 12 months, which reflects a challenging year for ALT5 Sigma. Currently, it is near the lower end of its 52-week range, suggesting that it has struggled to maintain higher price levels.

This collaboration with Nano Labs could enhance ALT5’s capabilities in the rapidly evolving AI space, potentially positioning the company for future growth amid increasing demand for AI technologies in financial services.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

ALT5 Sigma is slated to provide its next financial update on May 12, 2026 (estimated).

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 18 cents (down from N/A YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $5.51 Million (down from N/A YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a N/A Rating. No recent analyst actions available.

Price Action

Price Activity: ALT5 Sigma shares were down 1% at $0.84 on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data. Nano Labs shares are trading higher by 0.41% to $2.42.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: What's Behind The Drop In Trio-Tech International Stock?
data.description:
The pullback is playing out even as the Nasdaq is leading the market higher today, with traders also watching how fast momentum names can unwind after Trio-Tech was recently up 41% to $11.72 in a recent high-beta tape.

  • Trio-Tech Intl stock is feeling bearish pressure. What’s behind TRT decline?

Trio-Tech To Raise $10 Million For AI Investments

Trio-Tech announced the pricing of a $10 million registered direct offering, with D. Boral Capital LLC acting as the exclusive placement agent. The company says it plans to use net proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes, including strategic investments tied to AI and automotive growth opportunities.

The broader market is leaning positive, with the S&P 500 up 0.44% and the Nasdaq up 1.40% as Technology gains 2.38%. Trio-Tech's drop is happening while those benchmarks are rising, pointing to a stock-specific reaction to the financing news.

Shares Stay Above Key Averages

Trio-Tech is still holding a stretched uptrend on longer-term measures, but Friday's selloff fits the profile of a momentum stock that can snap back sharply when new supply hits the tape. The stock is trading 83% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 112.7% above its 100-day SMA, which signals strong trend control by buyers even after the pullback.

The relative strength index (RSI), a momentum gauge, is 87.98, which is deep in overbought territory and often lines up with "air pocket" risk if sentiment shifts. RSI at 87.98 means the stock has been rising so fast that even small negatives can trigger fast profit-taking.

  • Key Resistance: $16.50 — near the prior peak area where rallies have recently stalled.
  • Key Support: $7.50 — closer to the rising short-term trend zone where dip-buyers have tended to show up.

Trio-Tech Leans Into AI Infrastructure

Trio-Tech International is a provider of reliability test equipment and services to the semiconductor and other industries, with customers spanning automotive electronics, industrial electronics, computing and data storage, consumer electronics, and communications.

It operates across Semiconductor Back-end Solutions and Industrial Electronics, with a footprint in the U.S., Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand and China. That mix matters today because the company is explicitly linking the offering proceeds to capacity expansion and growth opportunities tied to AI and automotive end markets.

Mega-cap earnings-driven risk appetite provides a benchmark for Trio-Tech because sharp moves in bellwethers often set the tone for small-cap momentum and financing-sensitive names. Tesla's quarter showed revenue of $22.71 billion (up 16% year over year) and adjusted EPS of $0.41, but the stock still fell over 1%, underscoring how quickly traders can fade "good news" when positioning is crowded.

Shares Slip On Friday

TRT Stock Price Activity: Trio-Tech Intl shares were down 9.22% at $12.51 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock
